mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad-based economic strength, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days in more than 50% of instances during a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation accelerates past 3%, it triggers market expectations of imminent monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve. This increases the discount rate for future earnings, causing negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical Fed responses to inflation crossing this threshold.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where the trigger condition is met over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in retail sales growth and a rise in the unemployment rate signals a severe contraction in consumer health. This joint signal precedes sharp downward revisions to GDP and earnings, leading to negative equity performance.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-mo avg change < 0 AND UNRATE 3-mo change > 0.2
failure_condition: The interaction signal's rank information coefficient remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production indicates that supply-side constraints are easing and corporate output is expanding to meet recovering demand, leading to positive earnings surprises and higher equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:The US economy is in a recovery phase post-2008 crisis.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the correlation between INDPRO 3-month momentum and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant and positive during NBER-defined recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, multi-month increase in the unemployment rate is a reliable leading indicator of a recession. This triggers a flight-to-safety trade, causing investors to sell equities and leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: In a walk-forward test, instances where the trigger is met are not followed by a negative median 60-day S&P 500 return.

mechanism_narrative: In a ZIRP environment, an increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) while the Fed Funds rate remains pinned at zero creates negative real yields. This erodes the value of fixed income alternatives and should push capital into equities seeking real returns.
failure_condition: The signal (e.g., CPIAUCSL 6-month change) has a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day forward returns during the ZIRP period.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic expansion. This directly translates into higher aggregate corporate revenues and profits, supporting higher equity market valuations.
failure_condition: The time-series beta of S&P 500 60-day forward returns to the latest quarterly real GDP growth figure is not positive and statistically significant over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a high-frequency signal of weakening consumer confidence and forward-looking investment, leading to a negative short-term market sentiment shock.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not negative with statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) over the full sample.
